headline: PRESIDENT OF INDIA IN ROMANIA; HOLDS BILATERAL MEETING WITH PRESIDENT OF ROMANIA AND LEADS DELEGATION-LEVEL TALKS
fullText:
President's Secretariat

PRESIDENT OF INDIA IN ROMANIA; HOLDS BILATERAL MEETING WITH PRESIDENT OF ROMANIA AND LEADS DELEGATION-LEVEL TALKS

ROMANIA IS AN IMPORTANT PARTNER FOR INDIA WITHIN THE EUROPEAN UNION; INDIA–EU FREE TRADE AGREEMENT WILL OPEN UNPRECEDENTED OPPORTUNITIES FOR OUR INDUSTRIES AND SKILLED PROFESSIONALS: PRESIDENT DROUPADI MURMU

23 JUL 2026 11:25PM by PIB Delhi

The President of India, Smt Droupadi Murmu reached Bucharest, Romania today (July 23, 2026), on the last leg of her State Visits to Moldova, North Macedonia and Romania. On her arrival at the Airport, she was warmly received by Ms Oana-Silvia Țoiu, Minister of Foreign Affairs of Romania.

This is the first State Visit by an Indian President to Romania in nearly three decades. Minister of State for Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution Smt. Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya, and Members of Parliament Dr. Dinesh Sharma and Shri Vijay Baghel are accompanying the President in this visit.

The President visited Cotroceni Palace, the official residence and office of the President of Romania. She was warmly received by H.E. Mr. Nicusor Dan, President of the Republic of Romania and was accorded a ceremonial welcome and guard of honour. During one-to-one meeting and delegation-level talks, the two leaders held productive and forward-looking discussions covering all major dimensions of India-Romania bilateral partnership. They agreed to further deepen cooperation across areas of priority for the two countries.

The President said that India and Romania have shared a warm and enduring friendship for over seven decades, founded on mutual trust, respect and strong people-to-people ties. Over the years, our partnership has steadily grown stronger and is now entering a new phase marked by greater ambition, stronger cooperation and a shared vision for the future.

The President said that economic cooperation remains an important pillar of the India-Romania partnership. The two leaders agreed to promote trade and investment, strengthen resilient supply chains, and encourage closer collaboration between the business communities of our two countries. They also agreed to work towards doubling our bilateral trade over the next three years.

The President said that Romania is an important partner for India within the European Union. She underlined that the India–EU Free Trade Agreement will open unprecedented opportunities for the industries and skilled professionals of the two countries.

The President said that education, culture and people-to-people contacts remain the foundation of India–Romania relations. She was happy to note that three important agreements in these areas were signed today.

The President said that India and Romania will expand joint research and development under the Programme of Cooperation in Science and Technology. An Indian Studies Chair is being established at the University of Bucharest with the support of the Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR). The Government of India has decided to double the number of training opportunities for Romania under the Indian Technical and Economic Cooperation (ITEC) Programme.

The President said that as India and Romania prepare to celebrate the 80th anniversary of the establishment of diplomatic relations in 2028, the two sides have agreed to commemorate the occasion as the "India–Romania Year of Innovation."

Both Presidents issued statements to the press. (Text of press statement by President Droupadi Murmu is attached).

Tomorrow (July 24, 2026), the President will address the India-Romania Business Forum and visit the Parliament of Romania and the Bucharest University of Economic Studies.

(Release ID: 2288689)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
President of India Droupadi Murmu arrived in Bucharest, Romania, on July 23, 2026, for a State Visit to hold bilateral talks with Romanian President Nicusor Dan. The visit aims to deepen bilateral cooperation and mark a new phase in the seven-decade-old partnership between the two nations.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* **Bilateral Economic Goals:** The leaders agreed to work toward doubling bilateral trade over the next three years and focused on promoting trade, investment, and resilient supply chains.
* **Diplomatic Milestones:** The two nations agreed to commemorate the 80th anniversary of their diplomatic relations in 2028 as the "India–Romania Year of Innovation."
* **Education and Culture:** 
    * Three new agreements were signed in the areas of education, culture, and people-to-people contacts.
    * An Indian Studies Chair is being established at the University of Bucharest with support from the Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR).
    * The Indian Technical and Economic Cooperation (ITEC) Programme will double the number of training opportunities for Romania.
* **Science and Technology:** The two countries agreed to expand joint research and development under the Programme of Cooperation in Science and Technology.
* **State Visit Context:** This marks the first State Visit by an Indian President to Romania in nearly three decades; the visit follows previous stops in Moldova and North Macedonia.
* **Upcoming Schedule:** On July 24, 2026, the President is scheduled to address the India-Romania Business Forum and visit the Parliament of Romania and the Bucharest University of Economic Studies.

headline: NRDC and NCISM Signed MoU to Promote Innovation, Intellectual Property Management and Commercialization in the Indian System of Medicine
fullText:
Ministry of Science & Technology

NRDC and NCISM Signed MoU to Promote Innovation, Intellectual Property Management and Commercialization in the Indian System of Medicine

23 JUL 2026 9:21PM by PIB Delhi

The National Research Development Corporation (NRDC) and the National Commission for Indian System of Medicine (NCISM), a statutory body under the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to establish a strategic partnership for promoting innovation, intellectual property (IP) management, technology transfer and commercialization of research outcomes in the Indian System of Medicine.

The Memorandum of Understanding was exchanged between Cmde. Amit Rastogi (Retd.), Chairman & Managing Director, NRDC, and Dr. Maneesha Kothekar, Chairperson, NCISM, in the presence of Dr. Allamprabhu Gudda, President, Board of Ayurveda (BOA); Prof. Asim Ali Khan, President, Board of Unani, Siddha and Sowa-Rigpa (BUSSR); Dr. Atul Babu Varshney, Member, Board of Ayurveda (BOA); and senior officials of NRDC and NCISM.

The MoU provides a comprehensive framework for collaboration to identify, protect, develop and commercialize innovative technologies and intellectual property generated by NCISM-regulated institutions and registered practitioners of the Indian Systems of Medicine. The partnership seeks to bridge the gap between research and commercialization by creating an enabling ecosystem for innovation-driven growth in Ayurveda, Unani, Siddha and Sowa-Rigpa.

Under the collaboration, NRDC will extend its expertise in intellectual property protection, patent and trademark facilitation, technology assessment, valuation, licensing, technology transfer, industry linkage and commercialization of technologies. The Corporation will also provide mentoring to innovators, facilitate incubation support, promote industry-sponsored research, and assist institutions in developing effective intellectual property management practices and commercialization strategies.

NCISM will facilitate engagement between NRDC and its regulated institutions, Research, Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development (RIED) Cells, and registered medical practitioners across the country. Institution-specific technical, commercial and financial engagements arising from the collaboration will be undertaken directly between NRDC and the respective institutions or innovators.

The collaboration will support a broad spectrum of activities, including identification and protection of intellectual property, patent and trademark filing, innovation mentoring, entrepreneurship development, institutional IP policy formulation, capacity-building programmes, innovation awareness initiatives, ideathons, hackathons, technology showcases, industry interactions, and the dissemination of research and innovation success stories. The partnership will also encourage the translation of academic and clinical research into validated products, technologies and services capable of addressing healthcare needs while contributing to the growth of the innovation ecosystem in the Indian System of Medicine.

Speaking on the occasion, Cmde. Amit Rastogi (Retd.), Chairman & Managing Director, NRDC, said that the collaboration would create a robust mechanism for supporting innovators and researchers working in the Indian System of Medicine. He noted that NRDC's expertise in intellectual property management and technology commercialization would help transform promising research into commercially deployable technologies, thereby enhancing societal impact and contributing to the vision of an innovation-driven and self-reliant India.

Dr. Maneesha Kothekar, Chairperson, NCISM, emphasized that the partnership marks an important step towards strengthening the research and innovation ecosystem across NCISM-regulated institutions. She stated that by integrating scientific research, innovation management and commercialization support, the collaboration would encourage researchers, faculty members and practitioners to develop evidence-based technologies and healthcare solutions rooted in India's rich traditional medical knowledge.

(Release ID: 2288636)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The National Research Development Corporation (NRDC) and the National Commission for Indian System of Medicine (NCISM) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to establish a strategic partnership aimed at promoting innovation, intellectual property (IP) management, technology transfer, and the commercialization of research within the Indian System of Medicine.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* **Objective:** To create a framework for identifying, protecting, developing, and commercializing innovative technologies and intellectual property generated by NCISM-regulated institutions and registered practitioners.
* **NRDC Responsibilities:** Providing expertise in IP protection, patent and trademark facilitation, technology assessment, valuation, licensing, technology transfer, industry linkage, mentoring, incubation support, and industry-sponsored research.
* **NCISM Responsibilities:** Facilitating engagement between NRDC and regulated institutions, Research, Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development (RIED) Cells, and registered medical practitioners across India.
* **Scope of Activities:** Includes patent and trademark filing, entrepreneurship development, institutional IP policy formulation, capacity-building, ideathons, hackathons, technology showcases, and industry interactions.
* **Target Areas:** Ayurveda, Unani, Siddha, and Sowa-Rigpa.
* **Operational Structure:** Specific technical, commercial, and financial engagements will be conducted directly between NRDC and the individual institutions or innovators.

headline: NIELIT and SCL Mohali Collaborate to Make Indigenous 1.2 µm Process Design Kit Available on eChipHub
fullText:
Ministry of Electronics & IT

NIELIT and SCL Mohali Collaborate to Make Indigenous 1.2 µm Process Design Kit Available on eChipHub

eChipHub serves as a comprehensive national platform for semiconductor design education and capacity building

23 JUL 2026 8:52PM by PIB Delhi

National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology (NIELIT), an autonomous scientific society under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, takes a significant step towards strengthening India's semiconductor ecosystem and advancing the objectives of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) by doing a collaboration with the Semiconductor Laboratory (SCL), Mohali, an autonomous organization under MeitY, to make the indigenous 1.2 µm Process Design Kit (PDK) available through eChipHub.

The initiative will provide students, researchers, faculty members, startups, MSMEs and industry professionals across the country with access to an indigenous semiconductor process technology for integrated circuit (IC) design and prototyping, thereby promoting practical semiconductor design education and innovation.

eChipHub has been implemented and is being managed by NIELIT under the MeitY-funded project titled "Development of EdTech Platform in Semiconductor Space to Democratize Chip Design and Development Ecosystem for Masses." The platform has been jointly developed by NIELIT and SoCTeamup Semiconductors Pvt. Ltd. and serves as a comprehensive national platform for semiconductor design education and capacity building. Under the ongoing MeitY-funded EdTech project, NIELIT aims to train nearly 30,000 students in semiconductor design through structured courses, bootcamps, faculty development programmes, internships and industry-academia collaborative initiatives.

As part of the collaboration, the SCL 1.2 µm Process Design Kit (PDK) has been integrated with eChipHub and will be made available through a secure Aadhaar-authenticated download mechanism, ensuring responsible access while encouraging wider adoption across the semiconductor ecosystem.

The integration of the indigenous open-source PDK with open-source EDA toolchains creates one of India's first indigenous design-to-fabrication learning pathways. It enables learners and innovators to undertake the complete semiconductor design workflow—from RTL design and simulation to synthesis, physical implementation and fabrication-oriented design—using indigenous process technology.

The collaboration further strengthens eChipHub by complementing its implementation-oriented courses, cloud-based virtual laboratories, Faculty Development Programmes (FDPs), workshops, internships, hackathons, Academic Alliance initiatives and collaborative chip design projects.

Through this collaboration, NIELIT and SCL are laying the foundation for an integrated design-to-fabrication learning and prototyping ecosystem, empowering students, researchers, innovators, startups, MSMEs and academic institutions to transform ideas into fabrication-ready integrated circuit designs using indigenous technologies, thereby contributing to India's vision of becoming a global hub for semiconductor design, innovation and product development.

On 25 April 2025, in the presence of Shri S. Krishnan, Secretary, MeitY, NIELIT signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Semiconductor Laboratory (SCL), Mohali to foster collaboration in semiconductor research and development, innovation, and skilling. The partnership aims to strengthen India's semiconductor ecosystem by promoting indigenous technology development, enhancing capacity building, supporting academia-industry collaboration, and creating a skilled workforce.

National Institute of Electronics & Information Technology (NIELIT), an Autonomous Scientific Society of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, has been a pioneer in skill development and digital empowerment.

With its extensive presence through 56 NIELIT centers, over 700 accredited institutes, and 8,000+ facilitation centers, NIELIT has skilled and certified millions of students in emerging technologies in the E&ICT domain.

NIELIT has been conferred the status of "Deemed to be University" under the distinct category by the Ministry of Education having its main campus at Ropar (Punjab) and eleven constituent campuses located in Aizawl, Agartala, Calicut, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Gorakhpur, Imphal, Itanagar, Ajmer, Kohima, Patna and Srinagar, aims to revolutionize higher education in E&ICT domain using digital technologies.

Dharmendra Tewari / Dr. Nayan Solanki / Kanishk Sharma / Vanshita Jaiswal

(Release ID: 2288610)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology (NIELIT) and the Semiconductor Laboratory (SCL), Mohali, have collaborated to make an indigenous 1.2 µm Process Design Kit (PDK) available via the eChipHub platform. This initiative aims to strengthen India's semiconductor ecosystem and support the objectives of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) by providing access to indigenous process technology for integrated circuit (IC) design and prototyping.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* **Platform Details:** eChipHub is a national platform for semiconductor design education and capacity building, jointly developed by NIELIT and Socteamup Semiconductors Pvt. Ltd.
* **Access Mechanism:** The SCL 1.2 µm PDK will be available through a secure Aadhaar-authenticated download mechanism.
* **Training Goals:** Under a MeitY-funded EdTech project, NIELIT aims to train nearly 30,000 students through structured courses, bootcamps, faculty development programmes, internships, and industry-academia collaborations.
* **Workflow Capabilities:** The integration of the open-source PDK with open-source EDA toolchains enables a complete design-to-fabrication learning pathway, covering RTL design, simulation, synthesis, physical implementation, and fabrication-oriented design.
* **Partnership Context:** An MoU to foster collaboration in semiconductor R&D, innovation, and skilling was signed on April 25, 2025, in the presence of MeitY Secretary Shri S. Krishnan.
* **Target Audience:** The initiative serves students, researchers, faculty members, startups, MSMEs, and industry professionals.
* **NIELIT Infrastructure:** NIELIT operates 56 centers, over 700 accredited institutes, and 8,000+ facilitation centers, and holds "Deemed to be University" status.

headline: India Concludes Eighth Trade Policy Review at WTO in Geneva
fullText:
Ministry of Commerce & Industry

India Concludes Eighth Trade Policy Review at WTO in Geneva

Commerce Secretary Reaffirms India's Commitment to Open, Transparent and WTO-Consistent Trade Regime

68 WTO Members Participate in India's Trade Policy Review, Reflecting Strong Global Interest in India's Economic Transformation

23 JUL 2026 8:49PM by PIB Delhi

The second and final session of India's Eighth Trade Policy Review (TPR) concluded today at the World Trade Organization (WTO) in Geneva. The Trade Policy Review is a key transparency and monitoring mechanism of the WTO under which Members' trade and related policies are periodically examined to enhance transparency, strengthen adherence to WTO rules and facilitate constructive dialogue among Members.

India's official delegation for the Eighth Trade Policy Review was led by Commerce Secretary Shri Rajesh Agarwal. In his closing statement to the WTO Membership, the Commerce Secretary thanked Members for their active participation and the keen interest shown in India's trade policies. He reaffirmed that reform is a continuous process and highlighted India's commitment to an open, transparent and predictable trade and investment regime. He underlined that India's tariff reforms, customs simplification measures and ambitious Free Trade Agreement (FTA) strategy have steadily strengthened the country's integration with the global economy while supporting domestic development objectives. He also emphasized that India remains a major engine of global demand.

Responding to the issues raised by Members, the Commerce Secretary reiterated that India's trade policies are guided by WTO-consistent principles while balancing the developmental needs of a large developing economy. He emphasized that India's agricultural tariff structure is designed to safeguard the livelihoods of millions of small, low-income and resource-poor farmers, ensuring fairness and equity, while industrial tariffs support supply chain resilience, diversification and domestic manufacturing capabilities in an evolving global economic environment.

During the review, India received 1,094 written questions from 44 WTO Members. During the two review sessions held on 21 and 23 July 2026, 68 WTO Members made interventions, reflecting growing global interest in India's economic transformation and its role in the multilateral trading system. Several Members recalled the value of their trade and economic relationship with India and broadly appreciated India's constructive engagement in the multilateral trading system and its strong economic resilience. Members also highlighted India's achievements in Digital Public Infrastructure, customs and trade facilitation modernization, innovation and startup initiatives, expansion of regional trade agreements, and efforts to integrate MSMEs into global value chains. They also welcomed India's acceptance of the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies.

On sanitary and phytosanitary measures, technical regulations and trade remedies, the Commerce Secretary reaffirmed India's commitment to transparency, stakeholder consultations and adherence to WTO rules. He noted that India's Quality Control Orders are intended to achieve legitimate public policy objectives, while trade remedy investigations are conducted transparently, based on objective evidence, due process and judicial oversight. He further highlighted that the overall trade coverage of India's trade remedy measures remains limited and that India continues to apply the Lesser Duty Rule in its anti-dumping framework.

The Commerce Secretary also highlighted India's continued efforts to promote sustainable development, women's economic empowerment, digital public infrastructure, intellectual property reforms, services trade, investment facilitation and Ease of Doing Business. He noted India's significant progress in financial inclusion, digital commerce, logistics modernization and environmental sustainability, while reaffirming India's commitment to an inclusive and innovation-driven growth model.

On WTO reform, the Commerce Secretary reiterated India's strong commitment to an open, inclusive, transparent and rules-based multilateral trading system. He emphasized that WTO reform should remain development-oriented, preserve policy space for developing countries, strengthen the Organization's credibility and deliver on existing mandates. India also reaffirmed its commitment to engaging constructively with all Members to strengthen the multilateral trading system while preserving the foundational principles of the WTO.

The Commerce Secretary expressed his gratitude to the Chair of the Trade Policy Review Body, Ambassador Nella Pepe Tavita-Levy of Samoa, and the Discussant, Ambassador Guilherme de Aguiar Patriota of Brazil, for their leadership and valuable contributions during the review process. He also thanked the WTO Secretariat, WTO Members, and the Indian teams in Geneva and New Delhi for their dedicated efforts in ensuring the successful conclusion of India's Eighth Trade Policy Review.

Concluding the review, the Commerce Secretary reaffirmed India's unwavering commitment to transparency, dialogue and constructive engagement within the WTO. Recalling the Indian philosophy of "Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam"—the world is one family—he emphasized that India will continue to work with all Members to strengthen the multilateral trading system so that international trade remains a force for shared prosperity, inclusive growth and sustainable development.

In his closing remarks, the Discussant, H.E. Mr. Guilherme de Aguiar Patriota (Brazil, in his personal capacity), observed that at a time of heightened global uncertainty, many Members expressed the expectation that India would continue to act as a bridge-builder by promoting dialogue, fostering consensus and strengthening a rules-based, inclusive and resilient multilateral trading system. He underscored India's unique position as a leading voice of the Global South and its important role in bridging differences among Members to advance collective action within the WTO.

In her concluding remarks, the Chair of the Trade Policy Review Body, Ambassador Nella Pepe Tavita-Levy of Samoa, noted that WTO Members widely acknowledged India's growing importance in the global economy and recognized its expanding role as a driver of global growth and trade. She also referred to the concerns raised by Members and expressed the hope that India would address them appropriately.

Overall, India's Eighth Trade Policy Review concluded on a positive note, underscoring the country's growing importance in the multilateral trading system and Members' expectation that India will continue to play a central role in strengthening and shaping the future of the rules-based global trading system.

Abhishek Dayal/ Ishita Biswas

(Release ID: 2288607)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
India concluded its Eighth Trade Policy Review (TPR) at the WTO in Geneva on 23 July 2026, reiterating its commitment to open, transparent, and WTO-consistent trade policies while emphasizing its role in global economic development.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **68 WTO Members** participated in the two-session TPR held on 21 and 23 July 2026, highlighting global interest in India’s economic policies.  
- **1,094 written questions** were received from 44 WTO Members during the review.  
- Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agarwal reaffirmed India’s focus on tariff reforms, customs simplification, and expanding its Free Trade Agreement (FTA) strategy.  
- Agricultural tariffs were designed to safeguard small and resource-poor farmers, while industrial tariffs aimed to bolster supply chain resilience and domestic manufacturing.  
- **Trade remedy measures** have limited overall coverage, with India applying the Lesser Duty Rule in anti-dumping cases.  
- Achievements in **Digital Public Infrastructure**, customs modernization, innovation, startup ecosystems, regional trade agreements, and MSME integration into global value chains were praised by Members.  
- India accepted the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies.  
- Commitments to transparency, stakeholder consultations, and adherence to WTO rules were emphasized in sanitary, phytosanitary, and technical regulation policies.  
- Progress in sustainable development, women’s empowerment, digital commerce, logistics modernization, and environmental sustainability was highlighted.  
- India reaffirmed its stance on WTO reforms, stressing a development-oriented approach that preserves policy space for developing countries.  
- The Discussant (Brazil) noted expectations for India to "act as a bridge-builder" amid global uncertainty, acknowledging its role as a leader of the Global South.  
- The TPR Chair (Samoa) underscored India’s growing global economic significance and its potential to address Member concerns.

headline: Successful development of first indigenous Expendable Turbo Jet Engine of 350 kg thrust class
fullText:
Ministry of Defence

Successful development of first indigenous Expendable Turbo Jet Engine of 350 kg thrust class

23 JUL 2026 8:19PM by PIB Delhi

The first indigenous Expendable Turbo Jet Engine of 350 kg thrust class, designed by DRDO’s Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE), has been successfully developed. GTRE had identified Azad Engineering, Hyderabad as Industry partner for the manufacturing and assembly of the engine. On July 22, 2026, the engine was successfully realised and delivered to GTRE by Azad Engineering, a landmark achievement for India's aerospace and defence ecosystem. The delivery marks the culmination of years of precision engineering, advanced manufacturing, and close partnership between India's scientific & industrial communities.

Mastered by only a handful of nations, jet engine technology is widely regarded as one of the most sophisticated engineering disciplines in the world, demanding exceptional precision, advanced metallurgy, and uncompromising manufacturing standards. Delivery of such complex system based on the design of DRDO reflects the growing technological capabilities of the Indian Defence Industry.

The engine was handed over to Distinguished Scientist & Director General (Aeronautical Systems) Dr K Rajalakshmi Menon and Outstanding Scientist and Director GTRE Dr SV Ramanamurty by CEO Azad Engineering Shri Rakesh Chopdar.

Defence Secretary and Secretary, Department of Defence R&D and Chairman, DRDO Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh complimented the partnership of GTRE and industry that has been instrumental in transforming the vision into a historic milestone.

(Release ID: 2288576)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
India’s Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) successfully developed its first indigenous expendable turbo jet engine with a thrust capacity of 350 kg, designed by its Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE) in collaboration with Azad Engineering of Hyderabad. The engine was delivered to GTRE on July 22, 2026, marking a significant milestone for India’s aerospace and defence sector.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **First indigenous expendable turbo jet engine** developed in India with a thrust class of 350 kg.  
- **Design**: GTRE, DRDO.  
- **Industry Partner**: Azad Engineering, Hyderabad.  
- **Delivery Date**: July 22, 2026.  
- **Handed Over To**: Distinguished Scientist & Director General (Aeronautical Systems) Dr K Rajalakshmi Menon, Outstanding Scientist & Director GTRE Dr SV Ramanamurty.  
- **Thrust Mastery**: Engine technology is mastered by only a handful of nations, highlighting its sophistication.  
- **Milestone**: Reflects growing technological capabilities of India’s defence industry and successful collaboration between GTRE and industry partners.  
- **Recognition**: Defence Secretary Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh acknowledged the partnership as instrumental in achieving this milestone.

headline: NEP 2020 is laying the foundation for an inclusive, skill-centric and future-ready education system – Union Minister of State for Education
fullText:
Ministry of Education

NEP 2020 is laying the foundation for an inclusive, skill-centric and future-ready education system – Union Minister of State for Education

Dr Sukanta Majumdar stresses on the importance of strengthening implementation of centrally sponsored schemes for achieving the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047

Two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers concludes

23 JUL 2026 8:19PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister of State for Education and Development of North Eastern Region, Dr. Sukanta Majumdar, today highlighted the transformative role of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in shaping an inclusive, skill-centric and future-ready education system. He was addressing the concluding session of the two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers to review Centrally Sponsored Schemes of the Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL), Ministry of Education, at the ICAR–National Agricultural Science Complex (NASC), New Delhi.

Dr Majumdar emphasised on the need to address existing challenges through collaborative efforts between the Centre and the States and UTs to ensure that every child, teacher and school were empowered to realise their full potential. He reiterated that the effective implementation of Centrally Sponsored Schemes is essential to achieving the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.

The Minister underscored the importance of PM POSHAN and Tithi Bhojan in improving the nutritional well-being of children, particularly those from marginalized communities. He called upon States and UTs to continue strengthening school education through coordinated efforts and sustained focus on quality, equity and inclusion.

The second day of the Conclave focused on key components of Samagra Shiksha and PM POSHAN under the PRAGATI platform. The sessions featured presentations, followed by suggestions and inputs from education ministers and officials of States and UTs. An open-house discussion was also organized on strengthening the implementation of centrally sponsored schemes, sharing best practices, and improving learning outcomes in school education.

The Conclave provided a valuable platform for the Centre and the States and UTs to share experiences, deliberate on implementation challenges and identify opportunities for further strengthening the delivery of centrally sponsored schemes. The deliberations reaffirmed the collective commitment towards ensuring equitable, inclusive and quality school education for every learner and accelerating progress towards Viksit Bharat 2047.

Presentations were made on the PM SHRI scheme by Additional Secretary, DoSEL, Shri Dheeraj Sahu; on ULLAS by Additional Secretary, DoSEL, Ms. Archana Sharma Awasthi; on inclusive education by Economic Advisor, DoSEL, Ms. A. Srija; on skill education by Joint Secretary, DoSEL, Ms. Prachi Pandey; and on PM POSHAN by Joint Secretary, DoSEL, Shri Asheesh Joshi. Joint Secretary, DoSEL, Dr. Amarpreet Duggal and other senior officials of the Union Ministry of Education, also attended the Conclave.

(Release ID: 2288577)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The Union Minister of State for Education, Dr. Sukanta Majumdar, addressed the concluding session of a two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers, emphasizing the role of NEP 2020 in building an inclusive, skill-centric education system. The Conclave focused on reviewing Centrally Sponsored Schemes and the collaborative implementation between the Centre and States/UTs to achieve the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
*   The Conclave was a two-day event convened to review Centrally Sponsored Schemes of the Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL).
*   The event was held at the ICAR–National Agricultural Science Complex (NASC) in New Delhi.
*   Key topics discussed included the PM POSHAN scheme, Tithi Bhojan, Samagra Shiksha, and the PRAGATI platform.
*   Presentations were delivered on the PM SHRI scheme, ULLAS, inclusive education, skill education, and PM POSHAN.
*   Ministers and officials shared best practices and identified opportunities to strengthen centrally sponsored schemes and improve learning outcomes.

headline: Shri Tokhan Sahu Reviews AMRUT 2.0's Shallow Aquifer Management Programme, Calls for Greater Community Participation under the 'Catch the Rain' Campaign
fullText:
Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs

Shri Tokhan Sahu Reviews AMRUT 2.0's Shallow Aquifer Management Programme, Calls for Greater Community Participation under the 'Catch the Rain' Campaign

870 Sites Mapped, 539 DPRs Worth Over ₹28 Crore Prepared Under SAM 2.0

23 JUL 2026 8:13PM by PIB Delhi

Reinforcing the Government of India's commitment to water conservation and sustainable urban water management under the 'Catch the Rain' campaign, Shri Tokhan Sahu, Minister of State for Housing and Urban Affairs, today chaired a review meeting with officials of the AMRUT Mission and representatives of the National Institute of Urban Affairs (NIUA) at Sankalp Bhawan, New Delhi.

During the meeting Shri Sahu reviewed the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs' contribution to the nationwide 'Catch the Rain' campaign through the Shallow Aquifer Management (SAM) Programme under AMRUT 2.0, under which the Ministry is supporting 75 Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across the country in planning and implementing scientific groundwater recharge interventions to strengthen urban water security and mitigate the risk of urban flooding.

The meeting was attended by Smt. Isha Kalia, Joint Secretary, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, Directors of the AMRUT Mission, and the Programme Specialist (Water & Environment) from the National Institute of Urban Affairs (NIUA). The officials briefed the Minister on the outcomes of the National Interaction with 75 SAM Cities, held on 20 July 2026, and presented the key learnings and progress emerging from cities implementing the programme.

Shri Sahu also reviewed the achievements and lessons from SAM 1.0, which was implemented as a pilot initiative in ten AMRUT cities between 2022 and 2024. The pilot programme demonstrated the effectiveness of scientific groundwater management through improved groundwater levels, enhanced institutional capacity and increased public awareness on sustainable water conservation practices, laying the foundation for the nationwide expansion of the programme under SAM 2.0.

During the review, it was noted that under SAM 2.0, a total of 870 sites have been mapped for key groundwater attributes, while 539 Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), with an estimated project value of over ₹28 crore, have been prepared to facilitate the implementation of groundwater recharge structures across participating cities.

Commending the progress made under the programme, Shri Sahu emphasised that India's approach to water conservation must combine scientific innovation with traditional wisdom. He encouraged officials to document and promote indigenous, community-led water conservation practices that have sustained local ecosystems for generations, alongside modern groundwater recharge technologies.

Emphasising the vision of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi that water conservation should become a "Jan Andolan" , Shri Sahu directed officials to strengthen community participation by actively engaging local communities, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), Resident Welfare Associations (RWAs), educational institutions and other grassroots organisations in the implementation of groundwater recharge initiatives. He underlined that public participation would be instrumental in ensuring the long-term sustainability and success of the programme. He further directed officials to ensure regular monitoring of SAM projects, document the best-performing cities, prepare a compendium of best practices, expedite the construction of groundwater recharge structures during the ongoing monsoon season, and identify innovative, scalable models that can be replicated across urban India.

Reaffirming the Government's commitment to building climate-resilient cities, Shri Sahu stated that the integration of scientific groundwater management, modern technology and active community participation under the SAM Programme will play a pivotal role in strengthening urban water security and advancing the objectives of the 'Catch the Rain' campaign.

(Release ID: 2288566)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs, through its Shallow Aquifer Management (SAM) Programme under AMRUT 2.0, is supporting 75 urban local bodies to implement scientific groundwater recharge interventions as part of the national “Catch the Rain” campaign. A review meeting chaired by Minister of State Shri Tokhan Sahu highlighted progress, achievements of the SAM 1.0 pilot, and directives to enhance community participation and monitor projects.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- SAM 2.0 is active in **75 Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)** across India.  
- **870 sites** have been mapped for key groundwater attributes under SAM 2.0.  
- **539 Detailed Project Reports (DPRs)** have been prepared, with an estimated project value of **over ₹28 crore**.  
- SAM 1.0 pilot ran in **ten AMRUT cities** from **2022 to 2024**, demonstrating improved groundwater levels, institutional capacity, and public awareness.  
- A National Interaction with the 75 SAM cities was held on **20 July 2026** to share learnings and progress.  
- Shri Tokhan Sahu urged documentation and promotion of indigenous, community‑led water conservation practices alongside modern recharge technologies.  
- He directed officials to:  
  1. Strengthen community participation by engaging local communities, Self‑Help Groups (SHGs), Resident Welfare Associations (RWAs), educational institutions, and other grassroots organisations.  
  2. Ensure regular monitoring of SAM projects.  
  3. Document best‑performing cities and prepare a compendium of best practices.  
  4. Expedite construction of groundwater recharge structures during the ongoing monsoon season.  
  5. Identify innovative, scalable models for replication across urban India.

headline: National Pledge Against Drug Abuse To Be Undertaken On 18th August 2026 Commemorating The 6th Anniversary Of The Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA)
fullText:
Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment

National Pledge Against Drug Abuse To Be Undertaken On 18th August 2026 Commemorating The 6th Anniversary Of The Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA)

More Than 29.68 Crore Citizens Reached Under Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan Through Nationwide Awareness Campaigns

23 JUL 2026 8:04PM by PIB Delhi

The National Mass Pledge Against Drug Abuse has been scheduled on 18 August 2026 to mark the 6th anniversary of the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA). The Union Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment is the Nodal Ministry that aims to mobilise 10 to 12 Crore+ citizens through both physical and online participation.

In this connection, the 2nd high-level coordination meeting chaired by Joint Secretary (Drug Prevention) in the Ministry Dr. Sandeep Rewaji Rathod, held today in national capitaldiscussed the plan and the comprehensive strategy to ensure the participation of the target number of citizens in nationwide pledge through physical and online mode in line with the objectives of Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan.

The meeting attended by the senior representatives from various ministries and departments, reviewed the ongoing work for the implementation strategy. The role of Line Ministries and Departments was discussed in mobilising officers, officials, students, parents, volunteers and other stakeholders for the nationwide pledge.

The meeting discussed organising pledge ceremonies across all affiliated offices, educational institutions, autonomous bodies, PSUs and field formations under respective Ministries and Departments including Railway, Defence, Police, CAPF, NCC, AYUSH, Rural Development, Revenue besides social, voluntary, spiritual organizations and social influencers.

The Uniform Reporting Mechanism for submission of participation details and outreach, was also discussed in the meeting. A power point presentation on the plan and coordinated strategy, was discussed threadbare which witnessed active deliberations by all the representatives from the Line Departments and the Ministries.

NMBA Pledge is an initiative to volunteer for reaching out to the victims of substance abuse. The pledge aims to encourage responsible behaviour to instil a sense of responsibility among people to stay away from drugs, to promote and foster sense of Unity and Commitment for encouraging stakeholders to work together towards a common goal. The Pledge further seeks to empower youth to contribute actively to developing a drug-free society.

The link of the NMBA e-Pledge is: https://nmba.dosje.gov.in/pledge-certificate?type=e-pledge

The meeting also highlighted to focus on the significant message to promote and encourage Drug De-addiction through Helpline—14446. The meeting focussed on the evolving spirit among citizens to act as volunteers by registering as Nasha Mukti Mitr through Department’s official website:

https://nmba.dosje.gov.in/volunteer/registration

The Union Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment launched the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA) on 15th August 2020 to combat drug demand in the country. Recognising the urgent need for collective action, the Ministry coordinates various initiatives including prevention, assessment, treatment, rehabilitation, aftercare, public information dissemination, and community awareness.

The Ministry is implementing a comprehensive and evidence-based strategy under the National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR). The strategy is based on the findings of the first National Survey on the Extent and Pattern of Substance Use in India conducted by National Drug Dependence Treatment Centre (NDDTC) at AIIMS, New Delhi.

Under NAPDDR, the Ministry provides financial assistance to State Governments, Union Territories, NGOs and Government hospitals for preventive education, awareness generation, counselling, treatment and rehabilitation.

As on date, the campaign has reached more than 29.68 crore citizens including over 11.43 Crore youth and 8.05 Crore women, through nationwide awareness activities. More than 1.25 Lakh Nasha Mukti Mitras (NMM) have also joined the campaign to strengthen community participation.

To improve accessibility to treatment services, the Ministry has launched the NMBA 2.0 Mobile Application enabling citizens to locate the nearest de-addiction centre.

The Government continues to strengthen its commitment towards a drug-free and socially inclusive India by expanding rehabilitation infrastructure, enhancing preventive interventions, simplifying implementation procedures and ensuring efficient delivery of Centrally Sponsored Schemes. Through sustained community participation, improved governance and technology-enabled service delivery, the Ministry remains committed to protecting vulnerable communities and promoting social justice for all.

PR-28-TAR/KV/GA-

(Release ID: 2288554)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The Union Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment has scheduled a National Mass Pledge Against Drug Abuse for 18 August 2026 to commemorate the 6th anniversary of the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA).

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* **Pledge Objectives:** To mobilize 10 to 12 crore citizens through physical and online participation to promote drug-free behavior and empower youth to assist in developing a drug-free society.
* **Target Reach Statistics:** The NMBA has reached over 29.68 crore citizens to date, including 11.43 crore youth and 8.05 crore women.
* **Community Participation:** More than 1.25 lakh "Nasha Mukti Mitras" (NMM) have joined the campaign as volunteers.
* **Implementation Strategy:** A high-level coordination meeting was held in Delhi to discuss mobilizing various stakeholders, including educational institutions, PSUs, and departments such as Railway, Defence, Police, CAPF, NCC, AYUSH, Rural Development, and Revenue.
* **NMBA Program Details:** Launched on 15 August 2020, the NMBA operates under the National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR) to provide prevention, assessment, treatment, and rehabilitation.
* **Key Resources:** 
    * Drug De-addiction Helpline: 14446.
    * NMBA 2.0 Mobile Application for locating de-addiction centres.
    * Official e-Pledge link: https://nmba.dosje.gov.in/pledge-certificate?type=e-pledge.

headline: Union Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sports Smt. Raksha Khadse chairs meeting on strengthening Maharashtra's sports and youth development ecosystem
fullText:
Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports

Union Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sports Smt. Raksha Khadse chairs meeting on strengthening Maharashtra's sports and youth development ecosystem

23 JUL 2026 7:59PM by PIB Delhi

New Delhi, July 23, 2026: Union Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sports, Smt. Raksha Khadse, chaired a meeting with senior officials of the Government of India and the Government of Maharashtra to deliberate on a comprehensive roadmap to strengthen Maharashtra's sports and youth development ecosystem.

The discussions focused on institutional reforms, high-performance sport, athlete development and governance, with the objective of positioning Maharashtra as a leading sporting state while contributing to India's long-term sporting ambitions.

The meeting was attended by Shri Pravinsinh Pardeshi, Chief Executive Officer, MITRA; Shri Hari Ranjan Rao, Secretary (Sports), Government of India & Director General, Sports Authority of India; Shri Ram Singh, Secretary, Sports Authority of India; Shri Ranjit Singh Deol, Principal Secretary (Sports), Government of Maharashtra; and Shri R. Vineel Krishna, Joint Secretary (Khelo India), Department of Sports, Government of India.

The deliberations covered a broad reform agenda aimed at creating an athlete-centric, professionally managed and future-ready sports ecosystem in Maharashtra. Discussions focused on institutional reforms in sports governance, strengthening high-performance systems, capacity building of coaches through structured Continuous Professional Development programmes, enhanced sports science support, robust athlete development pathways, strengthening Mission Lakshyavedh, comprehensive assessment of Khelo India Centres, sustainable sports infrastructure development, professional management of sports facilities, digital athlete management systems, greater participation of the private sector, international collaborations for knowledge exchange, reforms in the State's competition structure to ensure transparent and merit-based athlete progression, and the implementation roadmap for Project Mahadevaa.

The meeting also deliberated on measures to strengthen the Youth Affairs ecosystem through dedicated institutional mechanisms, enhanced youth engagement, leadership development and greater convergence among stakeholders.

Chairing the meeting, Smt. Raksha Khadse emphasised that close collaboration between the Union and State Governments is essential for achieving the objectives of the Khelo Bharat Niti and realising the vision of Viksit Bharat under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi. She noted that the proposed reforms would complement the vision of Chief Minister Shri Devendra Fadnavis to further strengthen Maharashtra's position as one of India's leading sporting states through institutional reforms, professional governance and sustained investment in athlete development.

The meeting concluded with a shared commitment to advancing these reforms through continued cooperation between the Government of India and the Government of Maharashtra, with the larger objective of building a globally competitive sports ecosystem and creating greater opportunities for athletes and youth across the State.

(Release ID: 2288550)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
Union Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sports, Smt. Raksha Khadse, chaired a meeting with senior officials from the Government of India and the Government of Maharashtra to develop a comprehensive roadmap for strengthening the state's sports and youth development ecosystem.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
*   **Primary Objectives:** Positioning Maharashtra as a leading sporting state and contributing to India's long-term sporting ambitions through institutional reforms, high-performance sport, and athlete development.
*   **Sports Reform Agenda:**
    *   Implementation of the roadmap for Project Mahadevaa and strengthening of Mission Lakshyavedh.
    *   Comprehensive assessment of Khelo India Centres.
    *   Development of sustainable sports infrastructure and professional management of facilities.
    *   Establishment of digital athlete management systems and reforms in the state's competition structure for merit-based progression.
    *   Enhanced sports science support and capacity building for coaches via Continuous Professional Development programmes.
    *   Increased private sector participation and international collaborations for knowledge exchange.
*   **Youth Affairs Focus:** Strengthening the ecosystem through dedicated institutional mechanisms, leadership development, and enhanced youth engagement.
*   **Strategic Alignment:** Coordination between Union and State Governments to achieve the objectives of the Khelo Bharat Niti and the vision of Viksit Bharat.
*   **Key Attendees:** The meeting included the CEO of MITRA, the Secretary (Sports) of the Government of India/DG of the Sports Authority of India, the Principal Secretary (Sports) of the Government of Maharashtra, and the Joint Secretary (Khelo India).
category: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports

headline: CSIR-NIScPR Released Technical Report on Human-Centered AI and Hosted Distinguished Lecture by Ms. Debjani Ghosh
fullText:
Ministry of Science & Technology

CSIR-NIScPR Released Technical Report on Human-Centered AI and Hosted Distinguished Lecture by Ms. Debjani Ghosh

23 JUL 2026 7:50PM by PIB Delhi

The CSIR-National Institute of Science Communication and Policy Research (CSIR-NIScPR), New Delhi, hosted Ms. Debjani Ghosh, Distinguished Fellow, NITI Aayog, for a Distinguished Lecture on "Key Trends Shaping the Tech Frontiers" on Tuesday. The lecture brought together scientists, researchers, academicians and students to deliberate on emerging technology trends, Artificial Intelligence (AI), digital innovation and India's evolving technology ecosystem.

On the occasion, Ms. Debjani Ghosh released the Institute's technical report titled "Human-Centered AI and Sustainable Development: Holistic Pathways for Energy Security" in the presence of Dr. Geeta Vani Rayasam, Director, CSIR-NIScPR, senior scientists, researchers, faculty members and invited dignitaries. The report brings together the key deliberations and recommendations emerging from the International Symposium on Human-Centered AI and Sustainable Development: Holistic Pathways for Energy Security, organised by CSIR-NIScPR as a Pre-Summit Event to the India AI Impact Summit 2026.

The report presents a Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HCAI) perspective that places human values, inclusivity, sustainability and responsible governance at the centre of AI development. It highlights AI's potential to strengthen smart grids, accelerate renewable energy integration, improve predictive maintenance and support decentralised energy systems, thereby enhancing energy security and system resilience.

Emphasising that technological advancement must be complemented by robust policy and governance mechanisms, the report advocates the integration of ethical safeguards, human oversight and Green AI practices to ensure that AI-enabled energy transitions remain equitable, trustworthy and aligned with long-term sustainable development goals.

The report also draws upon the vision articulated by the Hon'ble Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi, during the India AI Impact Summit 2026, where he presented the M.A.N.A.V. framework for Artificial Intelligence, comprising Moral and Ethical Systems, Accountable Governance, National Sovereignty, Accessible and Inclusive, and Valid and Legitimate, as guiding principles for responsible AI development and deployment.

Delivering the Distinguished Lecture, Ms. Debjani Ghosh shared valuable insights into the transformative technologies shaping the future, with particular emphasis on Artificial Intelligence, frontier technologies, digital innovation and India's rapidly evolving technology ecosystem. She highlighted the importance of building trusted AI ecosystems, strengthening innovation capabilities, nurturing digital talent and fostering collaborative governance to enable responsible technological advancement.

Ms. Ghosh also underscored the need to view emerging technologies through the lenses of inclusivity, resilience, sustainability and long-term societal impact. She emphasised that responsible AI, supported by collaborative policymaking and innovation-driven partnerships, will play a pivotal role in accelerating India's journey towards a knowledge-driven and digitally empowered future.

The lecture witnessed enthusiastic participation from scientists, researchers, academicians and students, leading to engaging discussions on AI governance, responsible innovation, frontier technologies and the importance of strengthening science-policy interfaces for informed decision-making.

The event reaffirmed CSIR-NIScPR's commitment to advancing evidence-based science, technology and innovation (STI) policy research and promoting informed dialogue on emerging technologies that support national development, sustainable innovation and responsible Artificial Intelligence governance.

(Release ID: 2288541)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The CSIR-National Institute of Science Communication and Policy Research (CSIR-NIScPR) hosted a Distinguished Lecture by Ms. Debjani Ghosh, during which a technical report titled *"Human-Centered AI and Sustainable Development: Holistic Pathways for Energy Security"* was released. The report advocates for a human-centered approach to AI development, emphasizing ethics, sustainability, and governance in alignment with India’s technology policy goals.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Event**: Distinguished Lecture by Ms. Debjani Ghosh, Distinguished Fellow, NITI Aayog, on "Key Trends Shaping the Tech Frontiers."  
- **Report Title**: *"Human-Centered AI and Sustainable Development: Holistic Pathways for Energy Security."*  
- **Date of Release**: 23 JUL 2026.  
- **Purpose of Report**: Emerged from the International Symposium on Human-Centered AI and Sustainable Development, a pre-Summit event to the India AI Impact Summit 2026.  
- **Core Focus**: AI development prioritizing human values, inclusivity, ethical governance, and sustainability for energy security.  
- **Key Applications**: Application of AI in smart grids, renewable energy integration, predictive maintenance, and decentralized energy systems.  
- **Policy Advocacy**: Integration of ethical safeguards, human oversight, and Green AI practices to align AI-driven energy transitions with sustainable development goals.  
- **M.A.N.A.V. Framework**: Guiding principles for AI development endorsed by Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi—Moral and Ethical Systems, Accountable Governance, National Sovereignty, Accessible and Inclusive access, and Valid and Legitimate deployment.  
- **Participants**: Scientists, researchers, academicians, students, and dignitaries engaged in discussions on AI governance and innovation.  
- **Outcome**: Reaffirmed CSIR-NIScPR’s role in advancing evidence-based science and technology policy research for national development.

headline: CAQM conducts Special Inspection of Urban Road Redevelopment works in Faridabad and Gurugram
fullText:
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

CAQM conducts Special Inspection of Urban Road Redevelopment works in Faridabad and Gurugram

Commission directs expediting Redevelopment Works and ensure Timely Completion in accordance with the prescribed Framework

23 JUL 2026 7:44PM by PIB Delhi

The Commission for Air Quality Management in NCR and Adjoining Areas (CAQM) conducted a special inspection drive in Faridabad and Gurugram on 22.07.2026. During the inspection, 64 road stretches spanning around 31 kilometres were assessed for compliance with the Commission's Standard Framework for Paving and Greening of Urban Roads.

For the inspection drive, the Commission deployed 9 Flying Squads to inspect road stretches falling under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Corporation Faridabad (MCF) and the Municipal Corporation Gurugram (MCG). The inspection covered 50 road stretches under MCF and 14 road stretches under MCG. Roads falling under the Right of Way (RoW) categories of less than 10 metres and 10-15 metres were covered during the inspection.

Of the 64 road stretches inspected, 49 road stretches (38 in Faridabad and 11 in Gurugram) were found to have end-to-end paving. However, end-to-end paving was not found on 15 road stretches, including 12 road stretches in Faridabad and 3 road stretches in Gurugram, indicating the need for completion of the remaining redevelopment works.

The Commission also observed that although MCF and MCG claimed that the roads had been redeveloped in accordance with the Standard Framework, instances of non-compliance were detected during the inspection. The concerned agencies are being communicated to identify the officers/officials responsible for furnishing incorrect information to the Commission and to take appropriate disciplinary action against them.

The Commission reiterated that such road stretches should be strictly and expeditiously (re)developed in accordance with the Standard Framework for Paving and Greening of Urban Roads. It emphasized that proper end-to-end paving of urban roads is one of the key measures for minimizing resuspension of road dust caused by vehicular movement and for enhancing the effectiveness of mechanized road sweeping and other dust mitigation measures. The concerned road-owning agencies have been advised to expedite the remaining redevelopment works and ensure timely completion in accordance with the prescribed Framework.

The Commission further emphasized that implementation of the Standard Framework by all road-owning agencies across Delhi-NCR remains critical for long-term mitigation of road dust. Inspection drives will continue across NCR to monitor on-ground implementation of various air pollution control measures and facilitate timely corrective actions wherever required.

(Release ID: 2288531)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
TheKwam Commission for Air Quality Management in NCR (CAQM) conducted a special inspection of 64 road redevelopment stretches in Faridabad and Gurugram on 22 July 2026, found gaps in end‑to‑end paving, and directed the municipalities to expedite and complete the remaining works in accordance with the Standard Framework for Paving and Greening of Urban Roads.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
- Inspection date: 22 July 2026; press release issued 23 July 2026.  
- 64 road stretches totaling approximately 31 km were assessed.  
- 9 flying squads were deployed: 50 stretches under the Municipal Corporation Faridabad (MCF) and 14 under the Municipal Corporation Gurugram (MCG).  
- Roads with right‑of‑way widths of less than 10 m and 10ctx‑15 m were included.  
- End‑to‑end paving was found on 49 stretches (38 in Faridabad, 11 in Gurugram).  
- 15 stretches lacked end‑to‑end paving (12 in Faridabad, 3 in Gurugram).  
- CAQM identified non‑compliance despite municipality claims and notified the concerned agencies for disciplinary action against responsible officials.  
- Municipalities were advised to expedite remaining redevelopment work and ensure timely completion per the Standard Framework.  
- CAQM emphasized that proper paving reduces road dust resuspension and improves the effectiveness of mechanized sweeping and other dust‑mitigation measures.  
- Inspection drives will continue across the NCR to monitor implementation and enable timely corrective actions.  

*(Release_WIDGET_ID: 2288531)*
category: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

headline: DRDO conducts successful maiden flight-test of ‘Kusha’ Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile
fullText:
Ministry of Defence

DRDO conducts successful maiden flight-test of ‘Kusha’ Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile

23 JUL 2026 7:37PM by PIB Delhi

Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) successfully conducted the maiden flight-test of ‘Kusha’ Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile from the APJ Abdul Kalam Island off the coast of Odisha on July 23, 2026. This maiden test was conducted against an electronic target simulating high-speed and high-altitude aerial threat which was successfully intercepted by the missile system.

The ‘Kusha’ missile system is capable of neutralising a wide variety of aerial threats including fighter jets, missiles, unmanned aerial vehicles and large enemy aircraft within a wide range & altitude envelope. All weapon system elements, including the missiles, radars, command and control centre, have been developed by various DRDO laboratories and Industry partners.

Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh stated that the successful test-flight of ‘Kusha’ marks an important milestone in Indian defence R&D, highlighting the capability in developing long range surface-to-air missile system, possessed only by a handful of nations. This, he added, will also eliminate the import dependency for such systems and signifies a giant leap in air defence capability of the nation.

Defence Secretary and Secretary, Department of Defence R&D and Chairman, DRDO Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh monitored all activities during the launch and congratulated all the team members involved in the successful flight-test.

(Release ID: 2288526)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
DRDO conducted the maiden flight-test of the 'Kusha' Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile on July 23, 2026, successfully intercepting an electronic target simulating high-speed and high-altitude aerial threats, marking a milestone in India's development of long-range air defense systems.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- Maiden flight-test of the 'Kusha' missile system held on July 23, 2026, from the APJ Abdul Kalam Island in Odisha.  
- Successfully intercepted an electronic target designed to simulate high-speed and high-altitude aerial threats.  
- Capable of neutralizing fighter jets, missiles, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and large enemy aircraft across a wide range and altitude envelope.  
- All components (missiles, radars, command and control center) developed by DRDO laboratories and industry partners.  
- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh highlighted the test as a milestone in Indian defense R&D, emphasizing reduced import dependency for such systems.  
- Defence Secretary Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh monitored the test and applauded the team involved.
